# Roadmapped

> Project management as flat files in your repo. Your backlog, roadmap and docs
> live as plain YAML and markdown inside your repository — the only source of
> truth. Driven by your AI agent through a CLI and a Claude skill. No database,
> no SaaS, no account. MIT licensed, free.

Roadmapped turns a git repository into its own project management tool. Tasks are
YAML files under `docs/tasks/`. The roadmap — done / available / locked — is
computed from the dependency graph on every read, never stored. The dashboard and
the CLI read and write the same files through the same validator; on error the
change rolls back, and task ids are never reused. Git history is the audit log.

It is agent-first: a CLI (`npx roadmapped`) and a Claude skill (which also runs as
an MCP server) let an AI agent create specs, tasks and dependencies in the correct
schema, and record what it ships (outcome, verification, commit). A git hook
refuses commits that aren't attached to a task. Roadmapped's own backlog is
managed by Roadmapped — the public repository is the proof.

## Key facts

- Data model: flat YAML/markdown files in the user's own repo. No database, no server, no telemetry.
- Work is classified by one of 9 fixed types — bug, feature, chore, brainstorm, design, marketing, communication, legal, business — not by stage or team.
- Priority is a computed "temperature" (downstream blockers + age + the type's base heat + an optional manual seed), never a fixed stage order.
- Interfaces: a React dashboard (Vite), a CLI (`npx roadmapped`), and a Claude skill that doubles as an MCP server.
- Knowledge base: `roadmapped init` builds a Graphify code/docs graph by default (`--no-kb` to skip), committed at `graphify-out/graph.json`; the agent queries it via MCP (`kb_neighborhood`, `kb_search`, `kb_node`) instead of grepping cold.
- Distribution: three doors, same wired state — npm (`npx roadmapped init`), the Claude Code plugin (`5e1y/roadmapped`), or straight from GitHub (`github:5e1y/roadmapped`). Auto-published to npm on every version bump; every install then keeps itself current from GitHub HEAD. Requirement: Node ≥ 22.18 and a package.json.
- License: MIT. Author: Rémi Courtillon.
- Privacy: data never leaves the user's machine; "delete account" is `rm -rf`.

## Links

- Home: https://roadmapped.dev/
- Source: https://github.com/5e1y/roadmapped
- Guide: https://github.com/5e1y/roadmapped/blob/main/docs/guide.md
- Public backlog (dogfooding proof): https://github.com/5e1y/roadmapped/tree/main/docs/tasks
